title: Project CARS 2

notes: The Game runs perfectly out of the box with no tweaks, smooth and fast. I get constant 60FPS on a mix of medium to high settings at 1080p Resolution. No problems affecting the Steam Play experience at all. Although the game has one common problem at full screen mode: every steam or system notification pop-up kicks you out of game and leaves you with a black screen - sound still running, forcing me to kill and restart the game. As someone else mentioned here, I added "-windows -borderless" to the game's launch options to force the game into borderless windowed mode; a feature that the developers failed to add and can cause windows users into problems too. It's not a problem of Steam Play, more of Xorg or what ever is used and I made the experience in different desktops like LXDE, that those black screened windows can be revived.
title: Project CARS 2

notes: Tested : Solo / Multi. Both runs perfectly

notes: An update back in October 2018 broke FFB, so I had to manually download an older version using the Steam Console, with the command "download_depot 378860 378861 4000032546361002812" Which downloaded a version that has working force feedback. The hardest part is getting Steam to not constantly try and update the game which requires editing the Steam appmanifest. Other than those two workarounds the game runs flawlessly at 1080p max settings. ( Super sampling set to High ) I get anywhere from 70-100FPS depending on the weather and track.
title: Project CARS 2

title: Project CARS 2

notes: Use these launch options AND leave VSync disabled in the game: -windowed -borderless -fpscap 62.8 That will fix a lot of lag and fix the alt tab problem. Game runs fairly well after using fps cap but it still stutters a little bit. To fix controller issues i set the controls to keyboard, unplugged my controller, closed game, replugged, started the game and went to controllers > xbox or custom your choice > Manually set each control with the controller. Only navigating the menu wont work with the controller.

title: Project CARS 2

notes:
I disabled Esync, and it seems to run pretty well. I have noticed performance is slightly lower than Windows, but acceptable and almost always above 60fps on high settings for me.

I also have a MOMO wheel so the force feedback wasn't working. I had to manually downgrade to a slightly older version of the game to fix it. To do that you need to go into the Steam console and type "download_depot 378860 378861 4000032546361002812"
This will download the correct version to your Steam folder. Just leave the console open and you will see the folder location when the download finishes. The only way to see progress is to watch the download page and you will see a download speed even when there is no game listed. Just be patient its about 50gb total. Once downloaded you just replace the current game files with the downloaded ones and force feedback works again. You can also edit the appmanifest file for PCars2 to disable Steam auto-updates.
title: Project CARS 2

notes: Works good with no tweaks, although I have noticed 2 issues so far. 1. The desktop mouse cursor appears in game sometimes and messes when menu selections when using a controller. The sollution is to move the mouse pointer off to the side of the screen so it doesn't highlight anything. No big deal. 2. For some reason, quitting the game makes Steam quit too. Other than that, performance seems pretty good! I'm running it at 1440p with a mix of high and medium settings and holding down 60fps pretty nicely so far, even in races with the maximum amount of cars and with various weather conditions.
title: Project CARS 2
